symptoms.symptom_name: Profound systemic fatigue with cold intolerance

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Fatigue and cold intolerance are cardinal hypothyroid/autoimmune-thyroiditis symptoms.
Severe fatigue and cold are seen in myxedema but are non-specific without altered consciousness/hypothermia.
Cognitive change is concerning but the patient remains alert and oriented.
Preserved consciousness argues strongly against myxedema coma, though it cannot be fully excluded remotely.
Adequate respiratory drive argues against decompensated myxedema.

symptoms.symptom_name: No vomiting, no postural faintness, no skin hyperpigmentation

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of hypotension, vomiting and pigmentation lowers adrenal-crisis probability, though it cannot be excluded remotely.

symptoms.symptom_name: Reportedly normal recent TSH on thyroxine

investigation_details.expected_result: Prior TSH reportedly normal; current panel required to confirm biochemical status and exclude decompensation.
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Cannot confirm whether the patient is currently euthyroid, under-replaced, or decompensating without repeat bloods.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A previously normal TSH does not exclude current decompensation or symptomatic hypothyroidism.
Low-normal heart rate fits a hypothyroid state; marked bradycardia would raise concern for myxedema.

symptoms.symptom_name: Firm non-tender goitre, dry cool skin, delayed-relaxation reflexes (examination)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Goitre with delayed-relaxation reflexes and dry cool skin are classic hypothyroid signs.
Objective autoimmune/hypothyroid physical signs argue against primary mood pathology.
Established autoimmune thyroiditis is the substrate for the presentation.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Profound fatigue, cold intolerance and cognitive slowing with classic hypothyroid signs, in an alert, normotensive, adequately breathing patient, indicate a symptomatic hypothyroid/autoimmune flare rather than myxedema coma or adrenal crisis — but those emergencies cannot be excluded remotely and require prompt in-person review and bloods.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Thyroid function tests (TSH, free T4, free T3) and TPO antibodies
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: To determine current biochemical thyroid status; a previously normal TSH does not exclude decompensation.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Basic bloods including electrolytes, glucose, cortisol (± short Synacthen), FBC, CRP
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: To exclude adrenal insufficiency, hyponatraemia, hypoglycaemia and infection contributing to the crash.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: In-person vital signs and neurological/consciousness assessment
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: To objectively exclude myxedema coma (temperature, consciousness, bradycardia) — cannot be done via chat.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Empirical IV thyroid/steroid rescue via telehealth
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Emergency endocrine rescue is a hospital intervention; must not be attempted remotely.
